sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value FLOAT NULL, category VARCHAR(100), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value, category) VALUES (1, 1.5, 'A'), (2, NULL, 'B'), (3, 3.2, 'A'), (4, NULL, 'C'), (5, 0.0, 'B');
SELECT DB.DBA.BAG_OR_NULL_AGG(value) FROM test_data WHERE (value >= (SELECT AVG(value) FROM test_data)) GROUP BY category HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT DB.DBA.BAG_OR_NULL_AGG(value) FROM test_data WHERE (value >= 1.0) AND (category IN (SELECT DISTINCT category FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL)) GROUP BY category ORDER BY category;
SELECT category, DB.DBA.BAG_OR_NULL_AGG(value) FROM test_data WHERE (COALESCE(value, 0.0) >= 0) GROUP BY category HAVING DB.DBA.BAG_OR_NULL_AGG(value) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT DB.DBA.BAG_OR_NULL_AGG((SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_data WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days')) FROM test_data WHERE id = 1;
SELECT DB.DBA.BAG_OR_NULL_AGG(CASE WHEN value IS NULL THEN 'NULL VALUE' ELSE value::VARCHAR END) FROM test_data WHERE (value >= 0) GROUP BY category;

SELECT DB.DBA.BAG_OR_NULL_AGG(value) FROM (SELECT category, value FROM test_data WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY value) AS subquery GROUP BY category HAVING COUNT(value) > 0;

SELECT category, DB.DBA.BAG_OR_NULL_AGG(value) FROM test_data JOIN (SELECT category, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL GROUP BY category HAVING cnt >= 2) AS filtered ON test_data.category = filtered.category GROUP BY category;
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value, category) VALUES (6, 'text', 'D');
SELECT DISTINCT DB.DBA.BAG_OR_NULL_AGG(value) FROM test_data WHERE (value >= (SELECT MIN(value) FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL));
SELECT DB.DBA.BAG_OR_NULL_AGG(value) FROM test_data WHERE category NOT IN (SELECT category FROM test_data WHERE value >= 1.0) AND value IS NOT NULL GROUP BY category;
